# awscc_outposts_site (Resource)

Definition of AWS::Outposts::Site Resource Type



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String)

### Optional

- `description` (String)
- `notes` (String)
- `operating_address` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--operating_address))
- `rack_physical_properties` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rack_physical_properties))
- `shipping_address` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--shipping_address))
- `tags` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tags))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Uniquely identifies the resource.
- `site_arn` (String)
- `site_id` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_outposts_site.example
  identity = {
    site_arn = "site_arn"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `site_arn` (String)

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_outposts_site.example
  id = "site_arn"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_outposts_site.example "site_arn"
```
